name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Tampa, Fl
description_short: NACA is a national non-profit housing counselor providing character-based mortgage lending, HUD-approved housing counseling, and homeownership programs with no down payments or closing costs.
description_long: NACA (National Community Reinvestment Coalition) has operated for 36 years as a non-profit advocate focused on closing the racial wealth disparity gap through accessible housing and lending practices. The organization has served 3 million people and helped 500,000 achieve homeownership, with $20 billion in mortgage commitments and involvement in 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally. NACA offers character-based mortgage lending that does not consider credit scores, combined with comprehensive housing counseling services delivered through in-person "Achieve the Dream" events held nationwide. Their mortgage products feature no down payment requirements, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and below-market fixed interest rates (ranging from 5% to 5.625% as of April 2026). What distinguishes NACA is their explicit mission-driven approach to equity and their rejection of traditional credit-score-based lending models. Rather than categorizing primarily as a mortgage lender, NACA functions as a non-profit HUD-approved housing counselor that also offers mortgages as part of comprehensive member services. Members access counseling, workshops on home buying and purchase processes, document upload portals, and status tracking through a member portal. The organization actively fights predatory lending and landlord practices in communities. While NACA has helped 75,000 people obtain mortgages, their scale relative to the broader mortgage market is limited. Prospective members must attend multi-day regional events and become "NACA Qualified" through their counseling process, which requires significant time commitment and engagement beyond typical mortgage application procedures.
pros: No credit score consideration—character-based lending open to people with poor or no credit history
No down payment and no closing costs or fees—eliminates major barriers to homeownership
No mortgage insurance required—reduces total borrowing costs compared to conventional mortgages
Below-market fixed interest rates—5% to 5.625% competitive with or below conventional market rates
HUD-approved housing counseling included—comprehensive financial education and homebuying guidance
Non-profit mission-focused organization—36-year track record of community advocacy and equity work
Member portal for document management and status tracking—transparent, accessible process management
cons: Multi-day in-person event requirement—prospective members must travel to regional 'Achieve the Dream' events (typically Friday-Sunday) to begin qualification process
Limited geographical availability—events only scheduled in select cities (Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis), not nationwide direct access
Time-intensive qualification process—becoming 'NACA Qualified' requires significant personal engagement and commitment beyond standard mortgage application
Scale limitations—75,000 mortgages issued is small relative to conventional mortgage market, suggesting limited availability and potential waitlists
No direct application online—must attend events in person; no indication of remote or fully online mortgage application option
best_for: First-time homebuyers with limited savings and no down payment funds
Individuals with poor credit history or no credit score who cannot qualify for conventional mortgages
People seeking non-profit, mission-aligned lending without predatory terms or fees
Homebuyers within geographic proximity to scheduled 'Achieve the Dream' event locations willing to attend multi-day workshops
diagnosis: NACA is best for first-time homebuyers with limited credit history or poor credit who have the flexibility to attend regional in-person events and want mission-driven, non-predatory lending. The primary caveat is that qualification requires attending multi-day weekend events in specific geographic locations, and NACA's limited scale may result in availability constraints compared to conventional mortgage lenders.
services: Character-based mortgage lending (no credit score consideration)
Member portal for document upload and file management
Status tracking system for loan applications
Achieve the Dream multi-day regional qualification events
Community advocacy against predatory lending and landlord practices
Volunteer opportunities and community engagement programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
